title: ceropegia conjuncta
intro: Ceropegia conjuncta, also known as the Indian string of hearts or Rosary vine, is a flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Ceropegia conjuncta has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cold, and skin problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and cardiovascular benefits. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and ointments.
